summary: Iteration 13 - Backend refactoring verification complete. Tested all 11 refactored route modules (auth, users, friends, keys, dm, servers, channels, roles, files, emojis, admin). 35/35 backend API tests passed. Frontend login and navigation verified working. Fixed MongoDB read_receipts index issue (changed from sparse to partial indexes).

minor.issue: MongoDB DuplicateKeyError due to sparse index not handling null values correctly - FIXED by changing to partial indexes

critical_code_review_comments: server.py refactored from ~2975 lines to ~220 lines - clean orchestrator pattern
All 11 route modules properly imported and mounted under /api prefix
deps.py contains shared dependencies (db, auth helpers, permissions) - good separation
WebSocket handler remains in server.py (correct - not a REST route)
CORS and startup/shutdown lifecycle properly maintained in server.py
Fixed read_receipts index: changed from sparse=True to partialFilterExpression for proper null handling
seed_data_creation: Multiple TEST_ prefixed servers created during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend refactoring from monolithic server.py to modular routes verified. All 11 route modules working: auth, users, friends, keys, dm, servers, channels, roles, files, emojis, admin. Fixed MongoDB read_receipts index issue - use partial indexes instead of sparse indexes for collections with nullable unique fields.

fix_applied.issue: MongoDB DuplicateKeyError on read_receipts collection when marking channel as read
fix_applied.root_cause: sparse=True index only skips documents where field is absent, not where field is null. When upserting channel read receipts, MongoDB creates documents with conversation_id: null which conflicts with the unique index.
fix_applied.fix: Changed from sparse=True to partialFilterExpression={'field': {'$type': 'string'}} which only applies the unique constraint when the field is a non-null string
